title: Verilator_coverage toggle coverage score miscalculates multiple points on one line
body:
# Issue

Verilator_coverage reports a very different toggle coverage score compared to VCS. I made the comparison on an open source SystemVerilog based design (i.e., OpenTitan AES block), and found a toggle score of 27% for Verilator while VCS reports 55.89%. I put all the details below on how I computed this score with Verilator. After investigating, I could identify one major difference on how Verilator_coverage computes the score. Verilator_coverage groups hit count based on the line number. Packed structure are then considered as a single signal in some cases, and the computed toggle coverage score is then very different. 

# Details 

For instance, in aes.sv the tl_i is an input of type tlul_pkg::tl_h2d_t containing multiple signals:
```
  input  tlul_pkg::tl_h2d_t                         tl_i,
```

The coverage.dat properly logs all the hit count for each field of the tl_i structure. Please, note that each fields of tl_i has its own logged hit count in coverage.dat and all share the same line number.
```
C '^Af^Bbuild/lowrisc_ip_aes_0.6/src/lowrisc_ip_aes_0.6/rtl/aes.sv^Al^B49^An^B53^Apage^Bv_toggle/aes^Ao^Btl_i.a_address[0]^Ah^BTOP.aes_tb.dut' 0
C '^Af^Bbuild/lowrisc_ip_aes_0.6/src/lowrisc_ip_aes_0.6/rtl/aes.sv^Al^B49^An^B53^Apage^Bv_toggle/aes^Ao^Btl_i.a_address[10]^Ah^BTOP.aes_tb.dut' 0
C '^Af^Bbuild/lowrisc_ip_aes_0.6/src/lowrisc_ip_aes_0.6/rtl/aes.sv^Al^B49^An^B53^Apage^Bv_toggle/aes^Ao^Btl_i.a_address[11]^Ah^BTOP.aes_tb.dut' 0
C '^Af^Bbuild/lowrisc_ip_aes_0.6/src/lowrisc_ip_aes_0.6/rtl/aes.sv^Al^B49^An^B53^Apage^Bv_toggle/aes^Ao^Btl_i.a_address[12]^Ah^BTOP.aes_tb.dut' 0
C '^Af^Bbuild/lowrisc_ip_aes_0.6/src/lowrisc_ip_aes_0.6/rtl/aes.sv^Al^B49^An^B53^Apage^Bv_toggle/aes^Ao^Btl_i.a_address[13]^Ah^BTOP.aes_tb.dut' 0
C '^Af^Bbuild/lowrisc_ip_aes_0.6/src/lowrisc_ip_aes_0.6/rtl/aes.sv^Al^B49^An^B53^Apage^Bv_toggle/aes^Ao^Btl_i.a_address[14]^Ah^BTOP.aes_tb.dut' 0
C '^Af^Bbuild/lowrisc_ip_aes_0.6/src/lowrisc_ip_aes_0.6/rtl/aes.sv^Al^B49^An^B53^Apage^Bv_toggle/aes^Ao^Btl_i.a_address[15]^Ah^BTOP.aes_tb.dut' 0
C '^Af^Bbuild/lowrisc_ip_aes_0.6/src/lowrisc_ip_aes_0.6/rtl/aes.sv^Al^B49^An^B53^Apage^Bv_toggle/aes^Ao^Btl_i.a_address[16]^Ah^BTOP.aes_tb.dut' 0

```

But verilator_coverage group hit count based on the source code line number. Meaning that the hit count for the structure fields is a single value and so reported as one single signal. 
File VlcTop.cpp:
```
void VlcTop::annotateCalc() {
            ....
            source.incCount(lineno, point.column(), point.count(), ok);
```

The line based approach seems to be valid for computing line coverage, however for toggle coverage the score is then very different than the score reported by VCS.

# What 'verilator --version' are you using?
Verilator version: Verilator 5.005 devel rev v5.004-58-g5fce23e90

# Did you try it with the git master version?
Same behavior.

# OS/distribution
5.15.0-57-generic Linux / Ubuntu 22.04.1 LTS

# May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?
If the issue gets confirmed, I would be glad to provide a fix.

# Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)

The lines below are required to patch the build script of the example. 
Saving the lines into diff.patch
```
diff --git a/infra/base-afl/compile b/infra/base-afl/compile
index 0b56d73..fd89683 100755
--- a/infra/base-afl/compile
+++ b/infra/base-afl/compile
@@ -130,7 +130,9 @@ make \
   VLTRT_CXX=$VLTRT_CXX \
   VLTRT_CXXFLAGS="$VLTRT_CXXFLAGS" \
   LINK=$LINK \
-  DISABLE_VCD_TRACING=1
+  DISABLE_VCD_TRACING=1 \
+  ENABLE_COVERAGE_TRACING=1 \
+  VM_COVERAGE=1
 echo "Done!"
 
 ################################################################################
diff --git a/infra/base-sim/common.mk b/infra/base-sim/common.mk
index b0b710a..65f2be0 100644
--- a/infra/base-sim/common.mk
+++ b/infra/base-sim/common.mk
@@ -71,7 +71,7 @@ VFLAGS += $(addprefix -I, $(HDL_INC_DIRS))
 endif
 
 ifdef ENABLE_COVERAGE_TRACING
-VFLAGS += --coverage
+VFLAGS += --coverage-toggle
 endif
 
 ifndef DISABLE_VCD_TRACING
diff --git a/infra/base-sim/tb/cpp/src/ot_ip_fuzz_tb.cpp b/infra/base-sim/tb/cpp/src/ot_ip_fuzz_tb.cpp
index 4fdb285..5a468bc 100644
--- a/infra/base-sim/tb/cpp/src/ot_ip_fuzz_tb.cpp
+++ b/infra/base-sim/tb/cpp/src/ot_ip_fuzz_tb.cpp
@@ -221,6 +221,7 @@ void OTIPFuzzTb::SimulateDUT() {
         }
 
         default: {
+          std::cout << "(ignore)" << std::endl;
           // Handles a kInvalid fuzz opcode
           break;
         }
```

```
git clone https://github.com/googleinterns/hw-fuzzing.git
git -C ./hw-fuzzing apply ../diff.patch
cd hw-fuzzing
export HW_FUZZING=$(pwd) && export PYTHONPATH="./:../:$(pwd)/infra/hwfp:$(pwd)/infra/base-sim/hwfutils"
python3 ./infra/hwfp/hwfp/fuzz.py hw/opentitan/aes/cpp_afl.hjson

compile
./bin/aes < ./seeds/auto_cbc_128bit_decrypt_1block.hwf
cat logs/coverate.dat
verilator_coverage --annotate . ./logs/coverage.dat 
```

Reported toggle score is 27%, while VCS reports 55.89% score.
```
Total coverage (191/702) 27.00%
See lines with '%00' in .
```
